final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a Neo-Chinese-style tea room interior, designed for quiet tea meditation and contemplation.

Style/medium: photorealistic architectural interior photography, ink-wash painting aesthetic translated into real space, restrained and contemplative in the spirit of contemporary Chinese interior design
Composition: mid-range shot at seated eye level, slight wide-angle lens, balanced symmetry with natural depth leading to the window
Subject: natural wood tea table (原木茶台) as centerpiece, Yixing purple clay teapot (紫砂壶) and matching tea cups arranged on the table, calligraphy scrolls (书法卷轴) mounted on the wall, a potted bonsai (盆景) beside the scrolls
Scene/backdrop: serene Neo-Chinese tea room interior; through the window, bamboo stalks cast soft swaying shadows (竹影婆娑) on translucent paper screens
Lighting/mood: soft diffused natural daylight filtering through shoji-style rice paper windows, gentle dappled bamboo shadow patterns on walls and floor, tranquil and meditative (禅意盎然)
Color palette: warm natural wood tones as dominant base, muted celadon and ink-grey as accents, soft white from paper screens, no saturated colors
Materials/textures: visible wood grain on tea table surface, matte unglazed clay texture of the teapot, smooth rice paper screen, aged ink on calligraphy scrolls
Avoid: over-bright studio lighting, harsh shadows, modern minimalist Scandinavian aesthetic, clutter, plastic surfaces, neon or artificial color casts
